title: PASS verdict recorded while the ship never landed on main (cycle-948) — ship phase must verify base advanced or record an explicit unlanded outcome + auto-queue salvage
why: Cycle-948 recorded PASS (recent-outcomes) and its commit 12bab1df (merge-rung2 scoped-review fix: mergerung2.go + composition_scoped_review.go, 91+/36-) exists ONLY on branch cycle-21f9f7ae-948 — the fleet ship pushed the lane branch but the work never reached main. Worse: the commit consumed (deleted) its source inbox item, and a later batch-boundary sync archived the item as loop-consumed — so the work is neither landed nor queued: silently LOST. cyclerun.go:243 WARNs only for the no-HEAD-advance case; branch-landed-but-not-main is invisible. This is the concrete instance of concurrency_verdict_surface #3 / dormant ADR-0057 merge-to-main gate.
how_to_apply: Floor extension in the ship phase (config/policy layer per phase_settings rules): after a cycle-class ship, verify the cycle's commit is reachable from the base branch (merge-base --is-ancestor <ship-sha> main). If not: record CycleOutcome=SHIPPED_UNLANDED (not PASS), auto-queue a salvage todo naming the branch+sha, and do NOT delete/consume the source inbox item (item consumption must be transactional with the main-tree landing, not with the lane commit).
evidence: git cherry origin/main cycle-21f9f7ae-948 => 1 unlanded (12bab1df); recent-outcomes.md 'cycle 948 PASS'; inbox item 2026-07-13T16-02-00Z-merge-rung2-scoped-review.json absent from queue; merge-tree --write-tree origin/main cycle-21f9f7ae-948 => CONFLICT (mergerung2.go diverged since)
